construct_verdict_reason: Most items directly measure perceived discrimination against indigenous people, but p20 measures general skin color discrimination without indigenous-specific focus, making it tangentially related rather than directly on-target.

items.reason: Measures perception of skin color discrimination in general society rather than discrimination specifically against indigenous people; conflates racial/colorism discrimination with indigenous-specific discrimination.
Directly measures recognition of racism in Mexico, which is foundational to perceiving systemic discrimination against indigenous populations.
Directly assesses whether employment discrimination against indigenous people is recognized as discriminatory, measuring attitudinal recognition of indigenous-specific discrimination.
Directly measures perception that indigenous identity creates systemic barriers to employment, capturing assessment of structural inequality affecting indigenous people.
